email.subject: Daily Journey: Walker Miles on www.skechers.com

review.raw_markdown:
---

# Skechers.com Site Review — Walker Miles
**Date:** March 29, 2026 | **Persona:** Walker Miles, 62, Boomer Male | **Yesterday's Score: 4/10**

---

## 1. Executive Summary

Same problems, different day. I went through the whole thing on my phone — browsed my way to a Slip-ins shoe I liked, tried to add it to cart, and ended up staring at an empty cart telling me to join Skechers Plus. Which I'm already a member of. The login screen never really showed up as its own page, the cart didn't hold my item, and when I searched "slip in," the site showed me women's shoes. I'm a loyal Skechers customer and the site treated me like a stranger.

---

## 2. Business Impact Score: **4/10**

No improvement from yesterday. Core purchase flow is broken. A motivated buyer like me walked away empty-handed.

---

## 3. What's Working

**Men's navigation menu is genuinely good.** When I tapped into MEN, I got a clean, organized list — Shoes, Shop by Activity, Comfort Technologies, and right there in plain text at the bottom: **Hands Free Slip-ins**. That's exactly what I came for. No hunting, no confusion. Big, tappable rows. Easy.

**The Hands Free Slip-ins category page is solid.** 258 results, clear BOGO 50% OFF banner at the top, width filters right up front (Wide Fit, Extra Wide Fit). That matters to me — I need wide. The filter buttons are large enough to tap without frustration.

**Product page communicates value well.** I could see the shoe, the $135 price, the 4-star rating from 8 reviews, and "Buy 1 Get 1 50% off!" — all above the fold. The Slip-ins technology badge was right there. I knew what I was looking at.

**ADD TO CART button is full-width and hard to miss.** It's a big blue bar stuck to the bottom of the screen. No complaints there.

**BOGO promotion is persistent.** It stays visible in the top banner through almost every screen. That's smart merchandising.

---

## 4. What's Weak

**Login is invisible.** Steps 1, 2, and 3 all looked identical — the same Spring Shopping Guide homepage. There was no separate login screen that I could see. No confirmation I was signed in. No "Welcome back, Walker." Nothing. I'm a Skechers Plus member and the site never acknowledged that.

**Cart is completely empty.** This is the big one. After going all the way to the product page and hitting Add to Cart, my cart showed zero items. "There are no items in your cart." And then it had the nerve to show me a JOIN NOW / SIGN IN prompt for Skechers Plus — the program I'm supposedly already enrolled in. That's a slap in the face.

**Size selection not pre-filled or guided.** When I got to the size selection screen, it just sat there with a warning triangle saying "Please select a size." No recommendation, no "your saved size is 10.5," nothing. For a logged-in loyalty member, that data should be there.

**Search results default to Women's.** I searched "slip in" — 1,017 results — and the first two products I saw were both labeled Women's. I'm a 62-year-old man. The site knows what I browse. Why am I seeing women's shoes first? This is the same problem as yesterday.

**Homepage doesn't feel like it's for me.** The hero image is a Spring Shopping Guide featuring what looks like a couple or woman in a casual setting. No men's content front and center, no Slip-ins callout, no "Welcome back" for a logged-in member. It's generic.

**No personalization anywhere.** Not on the homepage, not in the cart, not in search. I feel like a first-time visitor on every page.

---

## 5. Recommendations

1. **Fix the login flow.** There should be a visible sign-in confirmation — at minimum, my name or "Hi, Walker" in the header after logging in. If login is failing silently, that's a critical bug.

2. **Fix Add to Cart for logged-in members.** A member's saved size should pre-populate so items actually land in cart. If the session isn't persisting from login to cart, that's a session management bug that kills conversion.

3. **Filter search by gender context.** When a customer browsing Men's searches "slip in," the results should default to Men's. Surface a gender filter toggle prominently at the top of results.

4. **Show Plus membership status persistently.** Once I'm logged in, the cart page should show my rewards balance, free shipping status, and a "Welcome back" — not a JOIN NOW button.

5. **Feature men's Slip-ins on the homepage.** Even a secondary banner below the hero could say "Hands Free Slip-ins — New Arrivals for Men." Don't make me dig.

---

## 6. Bottom Line

Yesterday I scored this a 4/10 and I can't go higher today. The Men's navigation is genuinely well-built — "Hands Free Slip-ins" right there in the menu, clear width options, good product pages. That part works. But the whole back half of the experience collapsed: login didn't register, the cart ended up empty, and search showed me women's shoes. I'm a loyal Plus member and I couldn't even buy the shoe I wanted. That's not a minor UX polish issue — that's a broken purchase funnel.

**Score: 4/10** — No regression, no improvement. Same critical failures as yesterday.

---

## 7. Evidence

### Step 1 — Homepage
Loaded the Spring Shopping Guide hero — woman/couple in a spring setting, big SHOP NOW button. BOGO 50% OFF in a thin banner at the very top. Cart icon and navigation icons up top. Nothing here speaks to me as a 62-year-old man looking for comfort shoes. No personalization, no men's content visible without scrolling or tapping. The text on the hero ("SPRING SHOPPING GUIDE") is large and readable, but the banner text at the very top ("BOGO 50% OFF Applied at Cart") is tiny — I'd probably miss it.

### Step 2 — Dismiss Popups
Screenshot looked identical to Step 1 — same homepage, same hero. No popup was visible to dismiss. Either there were no popups today (good, less friction) or the automation captured the wrong moment. Either way, no interrupt to deal with.

### Step 3 — Log In
Again, same homepage screenshot. No login form visible, no confirmation of login, no "Welcome back" message. From my vantage point, I have no idea if I'm logged in or not. This is a serious trust issue — as a returning member, I should know my status immediately.

### Step 4 — Men's Category
This was the highlight of the whole journey. Clean flyout menu: MEN at the top, then Shoes, Shop by Activity, Comfort Technologies, Collections, Collaborations, Clothing & Accessories — each with a right-pointing arrow. Below that, quick text links: **Hands Free Slip-ins**, Wide Fit, Extra Wide Fit. The row height is generous, easy to tap. "Comfort Technologies" is right there as a category — that's exactly the kind of language that resonates with me. This navigation is well-designed for a phone.

### Step 5 — Men's Shoes (Hands Free Slip-ins)
Category page loaded properly. BOGO 50% OFF banner at the top. Page title "Hands Free Slip-ins" in large text. Descriptive copy explaining the Heel Pillow technology — useful. Width filter buttons (Wide Fit, Extra Wide Fit, Medium...) displayed as pill buttons — easy to tap. "258 Results" shown. Filter and Sort by at the bottom. Two shoes visible starting to load in the grid. This page is doing its job. I can tell right away I'm in the right place.

### Step 6 — View Product
Product page for "Skechers Slip-ins: Max Cushioning Arch Fit 2.0 - Azusah" — good, clear title. $135.00. Four stars, 8 reviews. "Buy 1 Get 1 50% off! Applied at Cart" badge in green. Slip-ins technology logo badge visible. Product image shows a light grey/white athletic shoe — looks comfortable. No personalization visible (saved size, loyalty points, etc.). The info I need is above the fold without scrolling. Clean.

### Step 7 — Add to Cart
Size selection grid showed all men's sizes from 7.0 to 14.0 in a 5-column grid. The boxes are decently sized for tapping. A warning triangle with "Please select a size" appeared — no size was pre-selected, which is what I'd expect for a new visitor, but as a returning member my saved size should have been pre-loaded. QTY defaulted to 1, which is fine. "Ship it" and "Check in-store availability" options visible. Big blue "ADD TO CART" button pinned to the bottom of the screen — easy to see and tap. The size prompt failure (no pre-fill) likely caused the cart to be empty at the next step.

### Step 8 — View Cart
Empty cart. "There are no items in your cart." Full stop. The Skechers Plus promo box took up half the screen — "Get Free Shipping & Returns" with JOIN NOW and SIGN IN buttons. This is deeply frustrating. I'm supposedly logged in as a Plus member. I should see my membership perks acknowledged here, not a recruitment pitch. The CONTINUE SHOPPING button was there, but at this point I'm already annoyed. Order Summary section visible below but nothing in it. Complete purchase flow failure.

### Step 9 — Search ("slip in")
Search returned 1,017 results with "SLIP IN" as the heading. The first two products visible were both labeled **Women's**: "Skechers Slip-ins: Arch Fit Arcade - See Ya There" and "Skechers Slip-ins: Contour Foam - Cozy Fit" — both Women's, both showing color swatches. Filter and Sort By options at top. This is the same regression from yesterday. A man searching "slip in" on a site where he's browsed Men's content should not see Women's products first. No gender filter was visible or applied by default. Frustrating and unhelpful.

---

## Technical Audit

## Technical Summary

9-step mobile journey audited across skechers.com (Salesforce Commerce Cloud / Demandware). Key findings: 33 axe violations including 4 critical, incomplete Web Vitals instrumentation, a cart page TTFB spike, third-party CORS failures from Stylitics, and a broken analytics tracking call on cart load.

---

## Accessibility

**33 violations total — 4 critical, 7 serious, 16 moderate, 6 minor.**

| Severity | Rule | Page(s) |
|---|---|---|
| Critical | `aria-required-parent` — ARIA role missing required parent | Homepage |
| Critical | `aria-required-children` — ARIA role missing required children | View Product |
| Critical | `aria-valid-attr-value` — Invalid ARIA attribute values | View Product |
| Critical | `button-name` — Buttons with no discernible text | View Product |
| Serious | `link-name` — Links with no discernible text | Homepage |
| Serious | `aria-dialog-name` — Dialog/alertdialog missing accessible name | View Product |
| Serious | `aria-input-field-name` — Input fields missing accessible name | View Product |
| Serious | `link-in-text-block` — Links distinguished only by color | Men's Shoes |
| Serious | `color-contrast` — Foreground/background contrast below WCAG AA | View Product |
| Moderate | `landmark-main-is-top-level`, `landmark-no-duplicate-main` — Multiple or nested `<main>` landmarks | Homepage |
| Moderate | `landmark-complementary-is-top-level` — `<aside>` nested inside landmark | Homepage, Men's Shoes |
| Moderate | `heading-order` — Non-sequential heading levels | Homepage |
| Moderate | `landmark-unique`, `region` — Repeated landmarks, content outside landmarks | Homepage, Men's Shoes |

**View Product is the worst page** — 5 critical/serious ARIA violations, likely tied to a modal or interactive component (size selector, quickview dialog) with incomplete ARIA implementation.

---

## SEO

- `lang="en"` present on `<html>`. Locale class `g-local-en_US` confirms hreflang targeting exists.
- No canonical, meta-robots, or structured data visible in the truncated HTML — cannot confirm presence or absence from samples alone.
- Multiple GTM containers firing (`GTM-TDRBQZ`, multiple `gtag/destination` tags) — verify no duplicate pageview events are inflating GA4 session data, which can corrupt attribution.
- Placeholder GTM ID `G-1234567890` present in `<head>` on all pages — this is almost certainly a misconfigured tag and will fire empty/invalid GA4 hits.

---

## Performance

| Step | TTFB | LCP | CLS | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Homepage | 63ms | — | — | |
| Log In | 76ms | — | — | |
| Men's Category | 76ms | — | — | |
| Men's Shoes | 71ms | — | — | |
| View Product | 55ms | — | — | |
| Add to Cart | 55ms | — | — | |
| **View Cart** | **498ms** | — | — | **8-9× higher than other pages** |
| Search | 45ms | — | — | |

**LCP and CLS were not captured** — the audit is missing the two most user-impactful Core Web Vitals. Instrumentation should be fixed before drawing conclusions on ranking/UX impact.

**Cart TTFB spike (498ms)**: Likely caused by server-side cart hydration — pricing, inventory, promotions resolved server-side per request. Investigate whether cart state can be cached or pre-computed. This is the most actionable performance issue in the data.

**Third-party script bloat on every page** (counted from `<head>` alone):
- Pinterest, TikTok (×2), Google Ads (×3), GA4, Bloomreach, Creative Clicks, Impact, Quantum Metric, Reddit, Yahoo (×2), Bing, Tune/Go2SDK, Monetate (×2), WebFontLoader, GTM

That's **~20 async third-party scripts** injected into `<head>`. Even with `async`, these compete for network bandwidth and main-thread time. Monetate in particular (`se.monetate.net`) loads a custom personalization bundle on every page, which will delay LCP.

---

## Mobile Optimization

- CSS custom property `--vh: 6.640000000000001px` is set inline on `<html>` — this is a JS-computed viewport height fix for iOS Safari's dynamic toolbar behavior. Acceptable, but the floating-point precision is unnecessary; `6.64px` is sufficient.
- WebFontLoader (`webfontloader.js` v1.6.28) loads the Mulish font family. The `wf-*-active` classes are applied synchronously on `<html>`, suggesting fonts are loaded before first paint — watch for FOIT on slow connections.
- TrustArc consent manager injects inline `@font-face` declarations (Source Sans Pro from `consent.trustarc.com`) — these fire on every page load before consent is given, which may have GDPR/CCPA implications depending on jurisdiction.
- Yahoo `ytc.js` is loaded **twice** (`vice-managed-src`) — duplicate tag, wasted request.

---

## Console & Network Errors

**CORS failure — Stylitics stylesheet blocked (2 errors, same resource):**
```
Access to CSS at 'https://web-assets.stylitics.com/style.css?base=hotspots&version=1f1e003a2c'
blocked by CORS policy: No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header
```
Stylitics is an outfit/hotspot widget vendor. Their CDN is not returning `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` for XHR-fetched CSS. This breaks the widget silently. Root cause is on Stylitics's CDN config — escalate to their support. The resource is being fetched twice (likely once via `<link>` and once via XHR fallback), both failing.

**422 — Cart analytics tracking failure:**
```
422 https://nova.collect.igodigital.com/c2/100007688/track_cart?payload=...
```
`igodigital.com` is Salesforce Marketing Cloud's collect.js. The payload `{"url":"...","user_info":{"details":{}}}` contains an empty `user_info.details` object — the server is rejecting the request due to missing required fields. This means cart events are **not being recorded in SFMC**. Check what `user_info.details` should contain (likely customer ID or email) and ensure it's populated before the tracking call fires.

---

## Recommendations

**Priority 1 — Fix broken tracking (business impact)**
1. Investigate and fix the SFMC `track_cart` 422 — cart abandonment data is lost. Ensure `user_info` is populated when the user is logged in; suppress the call when fields are absent rather than sending an invalid payload.
2. Remove or reassign the placeholder `G-1234567890` GA4 measurement ID — it is firing on every page.
3. Deduplicate the Yahoo `ytc.js` tag.

**Priority 2 — Accessibility (legal/compliance risk)**
4. Fix critical ARIA violations on View Product (button names, `aria-required-children`, `aria-valid-attr-value`) — these are likely a modal or tab component.
5. Resolve duplicate/nested `<main>` landmark on Homepage — this is a structural HTML issue, not a component bug.
6. Add accessible names to all dialog elements.

**Priority 3 — Performance**
7. Instrument LCP and CLS across all steps — current data is insufficient to assess Core Web Vitals for Google Search ranking.
8. Investigate cart page TTFB (498ms) — profile the SFCC controller handling `/cart/` to identify whether the bottleneck is promotion calculation, inventory checks, or session resolution.
9. Audit and consolidate third-party scripts via GTM — ~20 scripts in `<head>` is excessive. Low-value tags (duplicate Yahoo, dead GTM IDs) should be removed.

**Priority 4 — Stylitics CORS**
10. File a ticket with Stylitics to add `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://www.skechers.com` to their CDN response headers for `web-assets.stylitics.com`. Until fixed, all hotspot widgets on the site are broken.
